channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve developer diary
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

First-person aerial combat and the world of Strangereal.

Publisher Bandai Namco and developer Bandai Namco Aces have released an eight-minute developer diary for Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve.

Here is an overview of the developer diary, via PlayStation Blog:

In this developer diary video from Project Aces, we’ll take a look at how Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve is the culmination of 30 years of experience and expertise.

One of the first things Ace Combat 8 explores is your role as a pilot in the world of Strangereal. As the new leader of a squad based on the carrier Endurance, you need to win your team’s trust which Kazutoki Kono, the series brand director, explains.

Your character is at the heart of a deeply emotional story, where you move between daily life on the aircraft carrier and high-stakes missions among the clouds as the Federation of Central Usea struggles to survive with limited resources in a bitter war.

To truly immerse each player, Project Aces engineered an innovation for the Ace Combat series, first-person perspective during story scenes. New levels of immersion are possible not only due to this new perspective, but also the huge leap forward in photorealistic rendering of your crafts, the environment, and, most importantly, that big, beautiful sky that’s just begging for a battle.

Developer and producer Kosuke Itomi, Masato Kanno, and Keiichi Yamaguchi describe how the art team applied decades of experience and craftsmanship to the task of bringing each fan-favorite aircraft to life.

We’re also excited to share more about the exclusive internal “Cloudly” engine, the “Lumen” lightning system which is used to render glorious, light-sensitive clouds and weather, and how this system changes the game for anyone soaring into that wild blue yonder.

And of course, beauty isn’t everything. Producer Manabu Shimomoto explains how the team has been working on making Ace Combat 8’s dogfights an unforgettable pulse-pounding ride. You’ll hear about their philosophy of making this a flight shooting game that anyone can enjoy, from the first-time pilot to the battle-hardened ace, and Development Director Akira Yasui will talk about how the new tutorial system helps accomplish that goal as well.

This deveveloper diary shares the feelings of a team with 30 years of development experience. Every ounce of that passion and commitment has been poured into environments that will immerse you, a story that will inspire you, and gameplay that will thrill you. Please join us as we prepare to take off for an unforgettable flight.

Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam in 2026.

Watch the developer diary below.

Developer Diary

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/ace-combat-8-wings-of-theve-developer-diary
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Famitsu Sales: 3/30/26 – 4/5/26
item.content:encoded:
• PS4
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

Pokemon Pokopia remains the top seller.

Famitsu has published its estimated physical game software data for Japan for the week of March 30, 2026 to April 5, 2026.

Pokemon Pokopia for Switch 2 remained the highest selling game of the week, moving an additional 45,484 retail copies.

On the hardware side, Switch 2 sold 59,543 units, the Switch family sold 16,342 units, the PlayStation 5 family sold 13,539 units, and the Xbox Series family sold 932 units. Total PlayStation 5 hardware sales have surpassed 7.5 million, while total Switch 2 sales have surpassed five million.

Get the full sales charts below.

Software Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. [SW2] Pokemon Pokopia (The Pokemon Company, 03/05/26) – 45,484 (867,171)
  2. [SW2] Mario Kart World (Nintendo, 06/05/25) – 8,131 (2,888,474)
  3. [NSW] Minecraft (Microsoft, 06/21/18) – 5,186 (4,182,507)
  4. [NSW] Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection (Capcom, 03/27/26) – 4,956 (23,194)
  5. [NSW] Animal Crossing: New Horizons (Nintendo, 03/20/20) – 4,710 (8,415,833)
  6. [SW2] Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection (Capcom, 03/13/26) – 4,588 (58,077)
  7. [SW2] Super Mario Bros. Wonder – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Meetup in Bellabel Park (Nintendo, 03/26/26) – 4,324 (14,482)
  8. [NSW] Pokemon FireRed Version / LeafGreen Version (Download Card) (The Pokemon Company, 02/28/26) – 4,146 (15,292)
  9. [PS5] Crimson Desert (Pearl Abyss, 03/19/26) – 3,812 (40,185)
  10. [SW2] Animal Crossing: New Horizons – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Nintendo, 01/15/26) – 3,706 (98,786)

Rankings 11 to 30 will be announced on April 10.

  1. [NSW] Winning Post 10 2026 (Koei Tecmo, 03/26/26) –
  2. [SW2] Mario Tennis Fever (Nintendo, 02/12/26) –
  3. [PS5] Winning Post 10 2026 (Koei Tecmo, 03/26/26) –
  4. [SW2] Winning Post 10 2026 (Koei Tecmo, 03/26/26) –
  5. [PS5] Resident Evil Requiem (Capcom, 02/27/26) –
  6. [NSW] Handsome Laundering: the mystic lover (Edia, 03/26/26) –
  7. [NSW] Okayu Nyumu! R (Entergram, 03/26/26) –
  8. [NSW] Pokemon Legends: Z-A (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  9. [NSW] Super Smash Bros. Ultimate (Nintendo, 12/07/18) –
  10. [SW2] Pokemon Legends: Z-A – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  11. [PS5] Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection (Capcom, 03/27/26) –
  12. [NSW] Mario Kart 8 Deluxe (Nintendo, 04/28/17) –
  13. [NSW] TAITO Milestones 4 (TAITO, 03/26/26) –
  14. [SW2] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  15. [SW2] Super Mario Party Jamboree – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Jamboree TV (Nintendo, 07/24/25) –
  16. [NSW] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –
  17. [SW2] Kirby Air Riders (Nintendo, 11/20/25) –
  18. [NSW] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  19. [NSW] Super Mario Party Jamboree (Nintendo, 10/17/24) –
  20. [SW2] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –

Hardware Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. Switch 2 – 59,543 (5,011,059)
  2. PlayStation 5 Digital Edition – 12,141 (1,248,515)
  3. Switch OLED Model – 7,468 (9,526,329)
  4. Switch Lite – 4,807 (6,903,964)
  5. Switch – 4,067 (20,0271,045)
  6. PlayStation 5 Pro – 840 (341,548)
  7. PlayStation 5 – 558 (5,910,474)
  8. Xbox Series X – 525 (325,952)
  9. Xbox Series X Digital Edition – 278 (29,362)
  10. Xbox Series S – 129 (341,622)


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/famitsu-sales-3-30-26-4-5-26
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: World of Tanks: HEAT closed beta test set for April 16 to 20
item.content:encoded:
Wargaming will host a closed beta test for free-to-play tactical vehicle shooter World of Tanks: HEAT from April 16 to 20 across PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, PC (Steam, Wargaming Game Center), and Nvidia GeForce NOW, the developer announced. Registration is available now via the official website.

Here are the details:

Set in an alternate post World War II era, World of Tanks: HEAT reimagines armored combat, bringing the franchise’s signature gameplay to more dynamic and fast-paced battlefields. Players step into the role of elite Agents with unique, game-changing abilities and pilot a roster of experimental vehicles. Each tank brings its own tactical strengths, enhanced by deep customization with advanced weapon systems, armor modules, and visual upgrades.

During the closed beta, players will be able to explore a diverse lineup of tanks and Agents, supporting different playstyles across three distinct roles: Defender, Assault, and Marksman. The beta will feature four player-versus-player modes across eight maps:

  • Hardpoint (five-versus-five) – Capture, defend, and hold a rolling series of dynamically changing bases.
  • Control (five-versus-five) – Capture and hold a single zone.
  • Kill Confirmed (five-versus-five) – Destroy enemies and grab their tokens, or grab your allies’ dropped tokens to deny the other team points.
  • Conquest (10-versus-10) – Large teams and multiple capture zones make for a sprawling, dynamic war zone.

Watch a new trailer below. View the screenshots at the gallery

Closed Beta Test Trailer

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/world-of-tanks-heat-closed-beta-test-set-for-april-16-to-20
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rhythm Heaven Groove launches July 2
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Prep veggies to the beat.

Rhythm Heaven Groove will launch for Switch on July 2, Nintendo announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Nintendo:

Get in the groove!

Anyone can feel the flow in Rhythm Paradise Groove! Discover a colorful collection of funny little rhythm games, including music by renowned Japanese producer Tsunku♂.

You can do anything with rhythm!

No matter how strange the situation, the rules are always the same: just press buttons in time with the beat! Hop through hoops, catch flying vegetables, swing sledgehammers, and much more—perform surprising feats with the music as your guide!

Watch a new trailer below.

“Slice N Dice Kitchen” Trailer

English



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/rhythm-heaven-groove-launches-july-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
